area: SUPERBRAIN frontend (GAG demo/gag-orchestrator/src) — the 3D r3f "superbrain" UI / lab canon
purpose: The marquee operator-facing front of the AI-OS: a Next.js 16 + react-three-fiber 3D "superbrain" that renders the supervised cognitive system as a living organism voyaging through deep knowledge space. It binds the real backend (SSE turns, pheromone trails, metrics, audit hash-chain, earned-autonomy ledger) to a brain/galaxy/signal scene, a HUD with an in-experience approval recipe, and a fully synthesized sound engine. Design constitution (VISION.md): "AN AGENTIC AI-OS SUPERBRAIN CONSTANTLY MOVING FORWARD (MOTION) IN THE DEEP VAST KNOWLEDGE SPACE" — motion is the thesis; honesty (no decorative lies, honest dormancy/offline) is the recurring law. This `gag-orchestrator` is the LAB (canonical source); `npm run port` copies the "live set" byte-faithfully to the product `frontend/`.
architecture: Entry `app/page.tsx` dynamic-imports `WorkspaceCanvas.tsx` (SSR off) which owns experience state (mode/activity/sky/surface/boot/glEpoch) and wraps everything in `QualityTierProvider` (WorkspaceCanvas.tsx:27-33,67-258). The R3F `<Canvas>` mounts `SuperbrainScene` (3D) and — unusually — `SuperbrainHUD` INSIDE the canvas, which then uses drei `<Html>` + `createPortal` to escape back into a `#hud-portal-root` DOM node (SuperbrainHUD.tsx:818-1038). The nervous system is a tiny module-singleton pub/sub `cognitionBus.ts` (NOT zustand — `stores/` is empty; zustand survives only in `attic/`); every layer (scene, HUD, metricsStore, soundEngine, QualityTierProvider, galaxy) subscribes to the same `CognitionEvent` stream. `lib/aiosAdapter.ts` is the single backend boundary: `streamTurn` parses SSE (`step`/`text_chunk`/`human_required`/`code`/`alignment`/`earned_autonomy`/`error`/`done`) into bus events; `pollOnce` (20s) reads `/development/trails`+`/metrics`, samples `/audit/verify` every 5 polls and `/development/autonomy` every poll, pushing real series into `metricsStore.ts` (the single source of truth shared by HUD intake rows and brain RegionPins). Frame-coupled state (breath/burst/wave/hold/idle/camera-push) lives in module-level refs and ONE shared-uniform object `SCENE_UNIFORMS` written once per frame so cortex shader, aura shells, fireflies and wires breathe phase-locked (SuperbrainScene.tsx:143-184,1136-1252). PostFX is a single EffectComposer (Bloom→CA→GradePre→AgX→GradePost→Vignette→Noise) with custom log-space + soft-light split-tone effects (PostFX.tsx).
capabilities: Streams a REAL supervised backend turn from the command bar and narrates every frame: tool_call→agent-dispatch+region thought-wave, [VERIFY PASS/FAIL]→knowledge-acquired, swarm/role_pass→caste narration, alignment/code/earned_autonomy frames all surfaced (aiosAdapter.ts:78-305)

In-experience approval recipe: a human_required pause publishes 'approval-required', freezes the organism's breath amber, and ApprovalPanel renders the real diff/command; AUTHORIZE replays the turn with the server token, REJECT POSTs to /api/v1/approval/req and only claims 'rejected' when the server confirms (aiosAdapter.ts:188-363, ApprovalPanel.tsx:44-115)

Polls trails+metrics every 20s and drives the whole HUD from real data — link dot, measured latency, verified/candidate counts, intervention rate, avg tool calls; fires knowledge-acquired only on genuine reinforcement and a full 'SKILL MASTERED' synapse storm on candidate→verified promotion (aiosAdapter.ts:485-671, SuperbrainScene.tsx:1070-1094)

MemoryGalaxy: every real pheromone trail is a persistent GPU star placed deterministically by skill_id (brightness=strength, size=walks, red pulse=quarantine, flash on recall); honest dormancy — no trails, no stars (MemoryGalaxy.tsx:92-190)
Audit-chain tamper surfacing: shield button shows TAMPER + a sustained tritone alarm when /audit/verify returns valid:false (aiosAdapter.ts:573-594, SuperbrainHUD.tsx:948-968, soundEngine.ts:157-163)

Earned-autonomy visibility: AUTONOMY ⚡N topbar pill appears only once a class is truly earned; 'CAPABILITY EARNED'/'AUTONOMOUS ACTION' bus events (aiosAdapter.ts:256-268,601-621, SuperbrainHUD.tsx:874-884)

Fully synthesized WebAudio voice bound to the SAME bus: breath hum, acquisition tick, verification-red shadow, mastery arpeggio, directive whoosh, approval sus-chord/resolve, trail-weakened ebb, link fifth, tamper tritone — sovereign (silent until operator's SOUND click) with limiter and OS-interruption recovery (soundEngine.ts)

Operator-sovereign topbar row: FIDELITY (high/med/low), SKY (voyage/layered), SURFACE (web/organ flesh GLB), SOUND — each persisted, each changed ONLY by the operator's own click; canon defaults locked (WorkspaceCanvas.tsx:42-102, SuperbrainHUD.tsx:886-942)

GPU resilience: webglcontextlost grace window then Canvas remount via glEpoch; WebGLErrorBoundary fallback; idle attract-mode after 30s with autonomous reflection cascades (WorkspaceCanvas.tsx:119-153, SuperbrainScene.tsx:1204-1244)
True-boot: BootSequence races the backend for 1.6s and replaces lore statuses with real version/trail/verified-rate facts when it answers in time (BootSequence.tsx:46-143)
sophistication: Anatomical region color baking: per-vertex fbm-warped region weights (frontal/parietal/temporal/occipital/cerebellum) with pow-sharpened dominance, luminance floor, and sulci AO darkening baked into a shared 'color'+'objectPos' attribute that clone(true) shares across cortex/wireframe/aura/signal layers — so labeled knowledge lights the SAME lobe every time (SuperbrainScene.tsx:311-453,468-510)

ONE shared-uniform object (SCENE_UNIFORMS) written once per frame drives cortex onBeforeCompile injection, two NeuralAura shells, CorticalSignals fireflies and NervousSystem wires so breath/wave/burst/hold are phase-locked across every shader — genuinely 'moves as one body' (SuperbrainScene.tsx:143-184,1136-1252)

Thought-wave system shared in GLSL (THOUGHT_WAVE_GLSL) between cortex and fireflies, scheduled CPU-side with NaN-safe clamps, anchored to tool-kind (waveLabelForTool) so a real dispatched tool visibly lights the lobe that owns that work (CorticalSignals.tsx:29-49, SuperbrainScene.tsx:456-510,1196-1251)

customProgramCacheKey keyed by tier (`superbrain_v6_${tier}`) — a documented hard-won fix: a constant key made three.js reuse the first-compiled program for the whole session regardless of FIDELITY (SuperbrainScene.tsx:798-803)

Bloom luminance ladder co-designed with a hand-built tone pipeline: AgX moved into PostFX (composer forces NoToneMapping), GradePre log-space contrast + GradePost W3C soft-light split-tone + vibrance; every recall/packet color clamped <=0.5 to keep 2x headroom under the 1.0 bloom knee (PostFX.tsx, CognitiveGrasp.tsx:62-81,295-300)

Approval 'hold' is a whole-organism state machine: breath freezes mid-inhale, cortex/wires/aura tint amber and dim, the starfield clock dilates to 30%, the camera dollies in, and sound plays an unresolved suspended 2nd — a single product thesis (a supervised mind deferring) rendered across 5 subsystems (SuperbrainScene.tsx:1024-1031,1143-1194, CosmicBackground.tsx:113-137, NervousSystem.tsx:74-75, soundEngine.ts:117-122)
NervousSystem builds 115 Frenet-framed spiral TubeGeometries merged into one draw call with a hardcoded tabX to avoid 60fps geometry rebuilds (NervousSystem.tsx:96-149,171-334)
Remount-safe state seeding: HUD seeds approval/telemetry/link from the adapter singleton (which outlives the component) so a GPU-loss remount never drops a pending hold (SuperbrainHUD.tsx:577-591)

Discipline infrastructure: 37 golden PNGs (canon-v1 + per-feature), 15 puppeteer probes, a tripwired `npm run port` with an explicit 'live set' allowlist + GLB prune, and a house rule of seeded RNG everywhere (seededRandom) for honest screenshot baselines

purpose: The repo-root Python scripts are the operator's command-line control plane and one-off bootstraps that sit OUTSIDE the production aios/ package. They split into three families: (1) a real, sophisticated agent-coordination DB/CLI (agent_coord.py) that serializes Claude+Codex writers and hash-pins review handoffs; (2) operator-authorized HTTP drivers (curriculum_evidence_driver.py, swarm_demo.py, earn_demo.py) that exercise the LIVE product over its real /api surface under a hard fail-closed allowlist to produce auditable evidence; and (3) a LEGACY/Phase-3 memory+ingest+reset toolchain (hybrid_search.py, ingest_knowledge.py, ingest_update.py, vector_memory_setup.py, reset_audit_chain.py, pdf_util.py, extract_text.py) that predates and is now superseded by aios/memory and aios/security, operating on a stale separate DB consumed only by legacy_node/.
architecture: agent_coord.py is a self-contained SQLite control plane (agent_coord.py:61-122) over .aios/state/coordination.db (agent_coord.py:21) with tables tasks/leases/messages/verdicts/events; it serializes writers via a single 'worktree' lease row with TTL+heartbeat (agent_coord.py:312-364,366-389), balances builder assignment 50/50 (route, agent_coord.py:160-173), and hash-pins handoffs by SHA-256 over HEAD+tracked-diff+untracked-contents (tree_snapshot, agent_coord.py:143-157) so record_verdict refuses if the tree changed after handoff (agent_coord.py:537-539). The three HTTP drivers all target BASE=http://127.0.0.1:8000 (curriculum_evidence_driver.py:32) and stream SSE from POST /api/generate (curriculum_evidence_driver.py:131-187), gating every human_required pause through check_allowlist (curriculum_evidence_driver.py:81-96) backed by two regexes (curriculum_evidence_driver.py:38,43-45); swarm_demo.py and earn_demo.py import that driver's vetted helpers verbatim (swarm_demo.py:19-26, earn_demo.py:10) rather than re-implementing approval logic. The legacy memory scripts hard-code DB_PATH='orchestrator_memory.sqlite' + FAISS_INDEX_PATH='vector_index.faiss' (hybrid_search.py:18-19) and are invoked by legacy_node/memoryAgent.js via `python hybrid_search.py` subprocess (legacy_node/memoryAgent.js:14); the production path instead uses aios/memory/retrieval.py and aios/config DATA_DIR DBs (aios/config.py:99-103).
capabilities: agent_coord.py: full writer-lease lifecycle — route/claim/heartbeat/release/handoff/verdict/message/inbox/status/brief with fail-closed CoordinationError on every guard (agent_coord.py:705-766)

agent_coord.py: tamper-evident review — record_verdict recomputes tree_snapshot and refuses approval if the worktree changed after handoff, and forbids a builder approving their own handoff (agent_coord.py:535-539)
agent_coord.py: deterministic title->category routing via keyword table and 50/50 load balance with stable tie-break by AGENTS index order (agent_coord.py:36-46,160-173,176-182)

curriculum_evidence_driver.py: drives the real supervised chat loop through curriculum_seed.json, parses SSE, replays through approvals up to MAX_REPLAYS=10, and classifies turn outcome by FINAL [VERIFY PASS/FAIL] marker (curriculum_evidence_driver.py:152-169)

curriculum_evidence_driver.py: subcommands seed/status/run/reps/plan-proof/trails hitting /api/v1/development/* endpoints; reps resets sandbox files between runs for identical replay (curriculum_evidence_driver.py:240-256,302-309,349-364)
curriculum_evidence_driver.py: appends every approval/rejection/verify-marker/curriculum-diff verbatim to .aios/audit/curriculum-evidence-run.jsonl (log_event, curriculum_evidence_driver.py:50-54)
swarm_demo.py: drives swarm=true multi-worker build, reports caste roles and counts EARNED-AUTONOMY auto-grants, prints the autonomy ledger before/after (swarm_demo.py:49-114)
earn_demo.py: pushes create_file:training_ground/*.py to 'earned' over 5 verified single-agent turns then detects the auto-grant when approvals==0 and status=='earned' (earn_demo.py:23-40)
hybrid_search.py: BM25+FAISS+exp temporal-decay fusion (0.25/0.45/0.30) over orchestrator_memory.sqlite, JSON to stdout for the legacy Node agent (hybrid_search.py:56-69)
vector_memory_setup.py: drops+recreates semantic_memory and writes a fresh FAISS IndexHNSWFlat(384,32) (vector_memory_setup.py:11-27)
reset_audit_chain.py: opt-in, non-destructive archive-then-clear of tamper_audit_trail in orchestrator_memory.sqlite, gated on explicit --yes (reset_audit_chain.py:18-32)
pdf_util.py / extract_text.py: PDF->markdown bootstrap extractors that produced blueprint_text.md (pdf_util.py:11-43, extract_text.py:3-16)
sophistication: Hash-pinned review handoffs: tree_snapshot hashes HEAD + binary tracked diff + sorted untracked file contents, so a reviewer's approve/blocked verdict is cryptographically bound to the exact tree inspected; any post-handoff edit fail-closes the verdict (agent_coord.py:143-157,537-539) — a genuine anti-spoofing control, not a label

Single-writer lease with TTL+heartbeat and lazy expiry: _active_writer deletes-and-events an expired lease inside the read, and claim uses INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE for an atomic compare-and-swap on the 'worktree' row under BEGIN IMMEDIATE (agent_coord.py:230-248,328-346)

Defense-in-depth allowlist shared across all three live drivers via import rather than copy-paste: ALLOWED_FILE_RE constrains writes to bare training_ground/*.py and ALLOWED_CMD_RE constrains verifies to pytest on a single such file — no pip, no shell, no path traversal — and reject_and_abort sys.exits the whole run on first violation (curriculum_evidence_driver.py:38-45,81-96,99-110)

reset_task_files re-runs the allowlist on its own deletion targets before unlinking, refusing to delete a non-allowlisted path even though it authored the seed (curriculum_evidence_driver.py:248-256) — fail-closed even against itself

Turn-outcome classification mirrors the server's FAIL-dominant/final-verdict semantics rather than naive any-pass, and warns on the silent 'verified success but no curriculum increment' record-matching mismatch (curriculum_evidence_driver.py:152-160,271-273)

earn_demo/swarm_demo treat earned-autonomy as something to be OBSERVED firing (approvals==0 while status=='earned'), not asserted — the evidence is the absence of a human pause on a real write (earn_demo.py:38-39, swarm_demo.py:79-81)

hidden_findings: DB SPLIT-BRAIN: the root legacy scripts hard-code orchestrator_memory.sqlite while the production aios/ stack uses entirely different DBs (aios_memory.db, aios_audit.db, aios_approvals.db — aios/config.py:99-103). They are NOT the same store; the root memory tools operate on a parallel, stale database.

reset_audit_chain.py is essentially a no-op against production audit integrity: it clears tamper_audit_trail in orchestrator_memory.sqlite (reset_audit_chain.py:15,27), but the LIVE audit ledger written by aios/security/audit_logger.py lives in aios_audit.db. Even within orchestrator_memory.sqlite the live table uses entry_id (verified via PRAGMA — no AUTOINCREMENT id), so its `DELETE FROM sqlite_sequence WHERE name='tamper_audit_trail'` (reset_audit_chain.py:28) silently matches nothing.

swarm_demo.py and earn_demo.py have ZERO independent approval logic — they import check_allowlist/reject_and_abort/parse_sse from curriculum_evidence_driver (swarm_demo.py:19-26, earn_demo.py:10). The hard allowlist is single-sourced, so the 'same hard allowlist' claim in their docstrings is literally true by construction, not by convention.

agent_coord.py's control plane explicitly CANNOT wake an agent or replace human approval (stated agent_coord.py:3-6) — it is a passive disk lock + ledger; all enforcement is cooperative (an agent that ignores the lease is not blocked at the filesystem level).

tree_snapshot reads every untracked file's full bytes into the hash (agent_coord.py:151-157); on a large dirty tree this is O(repo) per handoff/verdict/status call and could be slow, but it is what makes the hash-pin spoof-resistant.

curriculum_evidence_driver.reject_and_abort swallows the rejection POST's RequestException (curriculum_evidence_driver.py:107-108) and still sys.exits — so a failed reject network call does not stop the fail-closed abort, but the server may be left with a dangling un-rejected approval token.

hybrid_search.py and the ingest scripts assume CWD is the repo root (relative DB/index paths); the production code uses absolute config.DATA_DIR paths — running these from a subdir silently creates a fresh empty DB instead of erroring.

purpose: The automated test suite that guards the whole ai-editor system: the Python backend (aios/) security/cognition/memory/self-analysis/autonomy/swarm logic, the React product frontend (frontend/src), and the 3D "superbrain" lab orchestrator (GAG demo/gag-orchestrator). It is the executable specification and regression net that lets a single supervised developer refactor a large cognitive system without silent behavioral drift.
architecture: Three independent suites. (1) Backend: 28 test_*.py files + a session-wide conftest.py that points AIOS_DATA_DIR at a throwaway temp dir BEFORE aios.config is first imported (tests/conftest.py:27-28), so the real data/ SQLite + FAISS are never touched; a separate golden/ harness freezes Self-Analysis T1 findings against a committed fixture (tests/test_golden_analysis.py:65, tests/golden/expected_findings.json). The dominant test pattern is a deterministic scripted fake chat/LLM client driving the REAL ToolAgent loop while a FakeRunner Executor exercises the REAL security gateway + scope-lock — so blocking/gating behavior is genuine, not mocked away (tests/test_tool_agent.py:1-6, tests/test_swarm.py:31-37, tests/test_earned_autonomy_integration.py:27-33). The API layer uses FastAPI TestClient with DI overrides so no Ollama/shell/sandbox is hit (tests/test_api.py:1-5). (2) Product frontend: 9 *.test.{js,jsx} vitest+RTL files colocated in frontend/src (sse, conversation, DiffView, ProposalsPanel, MessageBubble, LivePreview, Alignment*). (3) Lab: 4 vitest test files under GAG demo/gag-orchestrator/src/test driving the real cognition bus, SSE adapter, sound engine, and quality-tier governor (setup.ts is jest-dom wiring, not a test).
capabilities: Backend suite runs 458 collected, 457 passed + 1 skipped in ~60-86s (verified live via `.venv/Scripts/python -m pytest tests/ -q`); the single skip is the symlink-escape scope test, skipif sys.platform=='win32' because symlink creation needs privilege on Windows (tests/test_security.py:133-140)

Live-measured 90% line coverage of aios/ (5087 stmts, 510 missed) via `coverage run --source=aios -m pytest` — every one of the 40 source modules is imported by at least one test; lowest module is semantic.py at 81%, no module below 81%

Security spine is exhaustively tested: zone classification, prompt-injection (regex + vector-shield dual layer, incl. fail-safe-on-embedder-error), scope escape (absolute/relative/symlink/~/redirect-glued/semicolon-glued/bare-..), secret scan+redact, durable rate-limiter, and the critical fail-closed-on-internal-exception guarantee (tests/test_security.py:235-241)

Self-apply engine has 18 mostly-negative tests: concurrent-lock refusal, credential-like approver refusal, no-self-approval, frozen-core RED refusal, multi-file/foreign-path/escaping-target refusal, audit-fail block, snapshot-integrity-mismatch, honest rollback-failure reporting, atomic-restore failure (tests/test_self_apply.py:110-307)

Earned-autonomy is tested at BOTH levels: the ledger contract (evidence-gated grant, single-failure instant revoke, scope-bound signature class, secret-redacted signature) in tests/test_autonomy.py, AND end-to-end through the real ToolAgent turn loop incl. a distinct earned-autonomy audit-chain entry (tests/test_earned_autonomy_integration.py:115-139)

Stigmergy/brain-growth deeply covered (31 tests): trail evaporation with disuse, reuse-failure quarantine + direct-success restoration, reuse-factor asymmetry/bounds, signature dedup + 3 migration backfill/merge tests, curriculum held-out-verifier gating (tests/test_brain_growth.py:250-744)

Golden-regression harness proves it is load-bearing: a deliberately mutated complexity_threshold=999 must drift the finding set, asserting the golden comparison is not vacuous (tests/test_golden_analysis.py:95-102)

Data-isolation is itself asserted as a test: derived DB/index/rollback paths must live under the temp DATA_DIR and hybrid_search must short-circuit []  WITHOUT loading the embedder on an empty index (tests/test_data_isolation.py:27-52)
Product frontend: 9 files / 29 tests pass (verified `npx vitest run`); SSE frame parser, DiffView, ProposalsPanel apply/reject + RED-disabled, alignment panels

Lab orchestrator: 4 files / 26 tests pass (verified); cognition-bus fault isolation, SSE adapter replay incl. approval token redemption + LINK OFFLINE honesty, sound-engine 'failed verification never sounds like success', demote-only quality-tier ratchet
sophistication: Session-wide isolation exploits pytest's import ordering: conftest.py sets AIOS_DATA_DIR before aios.config binds its derived paths, eliminating the old per-test hybrid_search stub entirely — and a dedicated test (test_data_isolation.py) guards that this isolation holds (tests/conftest.py:1-18)

The 'real gateway, fake runner' pattern is consistently applied: fakes are only the non-deterministic edges (LLM chat, shell spawn, embedder), while the security/scope/audit machinery runs for real, so tests prove genuine refusal behavior rather than asserting mocked returns (tests/test_swarm.py:1-8)

Golden harness is self-validating (test_golden_comparison_is_load_bearing) and intentionally leaves 'uncovered' out of the golden by withholding coverage_data_path so the join stays dormant and reproducible (tests/test_golden_analysis.py:46, golden/conftest.py collect_ignore_glob keeps fixture tests out of collection)

Sound-engine test installs a minimal recording WebAudio mock and asserts exact oscillator frequencies — proving a VERIFICATION RED event never plays the 1318.5Hz success tick, encoding 'what you hear is what happened' as a behavioral contract (GAG demo/.../soundEngine.test.ts:96-108)

Negative-path density is unusually high: self_apply (18 tests, ~15 refusal/rollback), security (~32 incl. fail-closed + 5 shell-composition parametrizations), agent_coord (one-writer-lease fail-closed, hash-pinned handoff, builder-cannot-self-review), reflection (concurrency increment, positive-delta clamp) — this is adversarial, not happy-path, testing
completeness: ~90% (genuinely high, evidence-based). Basis: live coverage run = 90% line coverage of aios/ with no module below 81%; 512 total passing tests across the three suites (457 backend + 29 product + 26 lab), all green when run; every source module is exercised; the test bodies are dominated by error/refusal/edge paths, not happy-path assertions. The remaining ~10% miss is almost entirely defensive exception branches and a handful of untested HTTP endpoints, not core logic. The '457 passed' headline is REAL but it counts the backend only — the honest cross-suite total is 512 passing + 1 skipped.
gaps: Three live API routes have NO endpoint-level (TestClient) test, though their underlying logic is unit-tested elsewhere: GET /api/v1/development/autonomy and POST /api/v1/development/autonomy/revoke (ledger tested in test_autonomy.py but not via HTTP) and GET /api/v1/development/trails (stigmergy tested in test_brain_growth.py but not via HTTP) — verified by grepping tests/test_api.py: 0 references to 'development/autonomy', 'development/trails'

Lab + product suites are NOT run by the backend pytest invocation and have no shared runner — a developer running only `pytest` (or only the documented backend command) gets a green that says nothing about the 55 JS/TS tests; there is no single 'run everything' gate

debt: api/main.py is the single largest coverage hole: 743 stmts, 111 missed (85%), and at 66KB the test_api.py is correspondingly huge (67 tests) — several error/guard branches (lines 896-940, 1254-1286, 1655-1660) and the autonomy/trails endpoints are untested, so this file concentrates both the most code and the most untested code (aios/api/main.py)

Provider/integration realism debt: no test ever exercises a real subprocess, real container, or real model — the suite proves the WIRING and gating are correct but cannot catch a regression in actual shell-quoting, container flags reaching docker, or live SSE byte-framing from Ollama

purpose: The single source of truth for runtime configuration (every path, threshold, retrieval weight, model/provider choice, and security flag) plus the deployment surface: the FastAPI/uvicorn HTTP server, browser CORS, the sandboxed Docker executor image, the Vite (classic) and Next.js (superbrain/GAG lab) frontend build configs, and how secrets are kept off disk and out of git.
architecture: aios/config.py:1-381 is a flat module of `Final` constants, each populated by typed env accessors (`_env_str/int/float/bool/path/scope_roots`, config.py:34-86) so every tunable is overridable via `AIOS_*` env vars or a gitignored root `.env` loaded by python-dotenv at import (config.py:28). Filesystem state lives under a single gitignored `DATA_DIR` (config.py:95-110) created on import. The HTTP layer (aios/api/main.py:111-149) is a FastAPI app whose `lifespan` enforces a deploy policy (non-loopback host requires a >=32-char `AIOS_API_TOKEN`, main.py:89-94), inits both SQLite DBs, validates the execution backend, and optionally installs the vector injection shield; a CORS middleware (main.py:120-126) plus an auth middleware (main.py:128-149) gate `/api/*`, `/docs`, `/redoc`, `/openapi.json`. There is NO `uvicorn.run`/`__main__` entrypoint — the server is launched manually via `.venv\Scripts\python -m uvicorn aios.api.main:app --port 8000` (README.md:58). Provider selection flows config -> DI (`get_bedrock_client`/`get_ollama_client`, main.py:152-180) -> `_select_chat_client` (main.py:1030-1070) which routes `auto`->best local tool-capable model, `ollama.x`->local, any other id->Bedrock-or-503. The chat endpoint streams SSE (main.py:1098-1100, 1724-1728). Container isolation is built from config in aios/core/executor.py:192-269.
capabilities: Fully env-overridable config with safe typed fallbacks: bad ints/floats/bools silently fall back to defaults rather than crashing (config.py:40-61)

Local-first defaults need ZERO config: DATA_DIR auto-created on import (config.py:96), SCOPE_ROOTS defaults to training_ground playground (config.py:217-219), API binds 127.0.0.1:8000 with no token (config.py:303-307)

Provider selection: Bedrock auto-enables purely from AIOS_BEDROCK_REGION+model being set (BEDROCK_ENABLED, config.py:297); local Ollama default llama3.1:8b (config.py:251); 'auto' picks best installed tool-capable model per inferred task (main.py:977-997, 1044-1052)
Earned-autonomy is OFF by default and double-gated: AIOS_EARNED_AUTONOMY master switch (config.py:177) + AIOS_EARNED_AUTONOMY_MIN_SUCCESSES=5 (config.py:179); RED is never earnable
Deploy guardrail: non-loopback API_HOST hard-fails startup without a >=32-char token (main.py:89-93); all /api/* + schema routes require Bearer token when set, else loopback-only (main.py:128-149)

Sandboxed executor image (Dockerfile.executor) runs as nobody (USER 65534) with empty ENTRYPOINT; the runtime adds --network none, --read-only, --cap-drop ALL, no-new-privileges, pids/memory/cpu limits, noexec tmpfs, single rw bind mount (executor.py:197-228)

Secrets kept off disk: Bedrock auth read only from env (AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK), never persisted (config.py:285-288); executor strips HOME/USERPROFILE/AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK and any KEY/TOKEN/SECRET/PASSWORD/BEARER-named var before spawning children (executor.py:49-51,272-281)
CORS pre-allows the four loopback dev/preview origins (Vite 5173 + preview 4173) and is comma-extendable via AIOS_CORS_ORIGINS (config.py:312-320)

Two frontends: classic Vite/React 19 (frontend/package.json) with a `showcase` build+preview script, and the Next.js 16 GAG superbrain lab (GAG demo/gag-orchestrator) with security headers + GLSL raw-loader rules (next.config.ts)
sophistication: Defense-in-depth env sanitisation for child processes: a name-substring blocklist (KEY/TOKEN/SECRET/PASSWORD/PASSWD/CREDENTIAL/BEARER) plus an explicit strip list, AND deterministic venv PATH prepend so bare `python`/`pytest` verify commands stay scope-legal regardless of how uvicorn was launched (executor.py:49-51,272-289)

The Docker runner is genuinely hardened, not theatre: no network, read-only root, all caps dropped, no-new-privileges, pid/mem/cpu caps, a 64m noexec/nosuid/nodev tmpfs, runs as 65534, and disables pytest cache writes via PYTEST_ADDOPTS (executor.py:201-225); fail-closed via UnavailableIsolationRunner for any unknown backend (executor.py:240-260)
Token check uses secrets.compare_digest for constant-time comparison (main.py:140), and OPTIONS is exempted so CORS preflight still works behind auth (main.py:136)

Vite aliases '@' INTO src/superbrain and shims process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_AIOS_URL at build time so the operator's Next.js lab files stay byte-identical when ported into the Vite app (vite.config.js:11-25) — a real cross-framework portability mechanism

Self-apply verification gets its own Executor with a fixed project-root pytest command and a guard that rejects any command other than DEFAULT_VERIFY_COMMAND (main.py:210-245), so the human-gated self-edit path can't be repurposed to run arbitrary code at project root
Injection vector shield install is best-effort and never blocks startup — a model/load failure is swallowed so the regex gateway stays the active defence (main.py:100-107)
completeness: ~85% for a local-first single-laptop target; ~55% for true multi-user/internet deployment. Basis: config coverage is exhaustive and well-documented; the loopback security model, sandboxed executor, and no-secret-persistence are production-grade for the stated single-operator use. The gap to general deployment is deliberate and documented (README.md:154-161 says browser bearer tokens are not multi-user identity and TLS must terminate in an external reverse proxy). No container/orchestration for the app itself, no CI config, no healthcheck wiring beyond GET /health (main.py:502-505).
gaps: No app-level deployment artifact: Dockerfile.executor is ONLY the sandboxed code-runner image, not an image for the API server or frontends. No docker-compose, no Procfile, no systemd unit, no process manager — the backend is started by a hand-typed uvicorn one-liner (README.md:58, START_HERE.md:60).

No uvicorn entrypoint in code: there is no `if __name__=='__main__'` / `uvicorn.run` anywhere in aios/ (grep found none), so port/host/reload are passed only on the CLI; AIOS_API_HOST/AIOS_API_PORT in config (config.py:303-304) are read by the policy check but NOT actually used to bind the server unless the operator manually mirrors them on the command line.

The superbrain frontend (the official default per project memory) sends NO Authorization header — aiosAdapter.ts only sets Content-Type (aiosAdapter.ts:194,342), and AIOS_BASE has no token plumbing (aiosAdapter.ts:21-22). Only the classic Vite config.js wires VITE_AIOS_API_TOKEN (config.js:4-5). So a token-protected/non-loopback deploy of the default UI would get 401s on every call.
No TLS/reverse-proxy config shipped; it is documented as an external operator responsibility (README.md:159-161) but nothing in-repo helps (no nginx/caddy sample).

No .env.example coverage for AIOS_EARNED_AUTONOMY, AIOS_SWARM_MAX_WORKERS, AIOS_INDEX_CHAT, AIOS_REFLECT_ON_FAILURE, AIOS_INTERPRET_ALIGNMENT, or AIOS_CORS_ORIGINS — several real flags are undocumented in the sample env (.env.example:1-58).

requirements.txt pins app deps but there is no separation of the heavy ML stack (torch 2.12, faiss-cpu, sentence-transformers, transformers) from the API/runtime deps, so the executor image installs the entire 2GB+ ML stack it never uses (Dockerfile.executor:13-14 installs all of requirements.txt).
hidden_findings: A LIVE AWS Bedrock bearer token is sitting in plaintext in the working tree at frontend/.env (frontend/.env:2,6 — `ABSKQmVkcm9ja0FQSUtleS...`). It is gitignored and verified NOT in git history (git ls-files shows it untracked; -S history search for the token prefix returns nothing), so it never leaked via the repo — but it is a real, currently-valid credential left on disk in a frontend dir that has nothing to do with Bedrock (Bedrock is backend-only). It should be rotated and moved to the backend env. Also note that file ALSO contains shell launch commands, so it's a personal scratch file misnamed as a frontend dotenv.

CORS is configured with allow_credentials=True AND allow_methods=['*'] / allow_headers=['*'] (main.py:122-126). With credentials enabled, wildcard methods/headers are reflected against the explicit origin list, which is fine for the loopback defaults — but if an operator adds a broad origin via AIOS_CORS_ORIGINS, credentialed cross-origin access widens silently. There is no validation that AIOS_CORS_ORIGINS entries are not '*'.

The bare `pytest` collection is restricted to tests/ via pytest.ini (testpaths=tests) specifically so a deliberately-broken training_ground sandbox during a BREATHE demo doesn't fail the suite (pytest.ini:1-6) — a non-obvious coupling between the test config and the agent's sandbox-breaking demos.

BEDROCK_ENABLED is derived at import from region+model both being truthy (config.py:297); since BEDROCK_MODEL has a non-empty default (amazon.nova-lite-v1:0), setting ONLY AIOS_BEDROCK_REGION flips cloud on. An operator who sets a region for some other reason would silently enable a cloud provider.

The frontend API base default is http://localhost:8000 (config.js:3) while the superbrain adapter defaults to http://127.0.0.1:8000 (aiosAdapter.ts:22). localhost vs 127.0.0.1 are DIFFERENT CORS origins; the CORS allow-list includes both localhost and 127.0.0.1 for 5173/4173 (config.py:312-320) so it works, but any new origin must be added in both spellings or break.

Dockerfile.executor sets ENTRYPOINT [] (executor.py image, Dockerfile.executor:18) deliberately so the runtime supplies the full argv; combined with --read-only root + noexec tmpfs the container cannot even write bytecode (PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE also set). This is intentional belt-and-suspenders, easy to mistake for redundancy.
debt: frontend/.env holds a live Bedrock credential plus shell snippets and AWS_REGION/AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK — wrong location (frontend, not backend), wrong content (a launch script in a dotenv), and a real secret on disk (frontend/.env:1-7). Rotate + relocate.

Two diverging frontend API clients with divergent auth + base-URL handling: config.js (token-aware, localhost) vs aiosAdapter.ts (no token, 127.0.0.1) — the official default UI cannot authenticate to a protected backend (aiosAdapter.ts:21-22,194,342 vs config.js:3-5).

Executor image installs the full ML requirements.txt (torch/faiss/transformers) it never needs, bloating the sandbox image build time and size for no benefit (Dockerfile.executor:13-14, requirements.txt:9-65).

config.py duplicates every constant name into a 59-entry __all__ (config.py:323-381) that must be hand-maintained in lockstep with the constants above — easy to drift (e.g. a new flag added but omitted from __all__).

No pinned/locked frontend deps beyond package.json caret ranges, and two separate node projects (frontend + GAG demo) with overlapping but version-skewed three/react-three stacks — drift risk across the port path.
gitignore parks several CSS files and a design PDF as 'source-reference artifacts' (.gitignore:41-49) — dead/parked assets tracked-out rather than removed.

area: Cross-Cutting Audit (dead code, security surface, tech debt, undocumented features, bundle weight)
purpose: A whole-repo "you'd never know unless you read it" net: surfacing orphaned/dead code, stale runtime artifacts, the security surface beyond the audited spine, performance hotspots, the ~1.3MB frontend bundle origin, and capabilities present in code but absent from the docs.
architecture: 262 tracked files across frontend/ (76), aios/ (46 — the live Python core), tests/ (40), training_ground/ (29 — the agent's writable sandbox playground), legacy_node/ (19 — the dead pre-Python prototype), and .aios/ (19 — the tracked Claude Code continuity brain). The live system is the Python aios/ package fronted by one FastAPI app (aios/api/main.py:1-1767, 31 routes, 15 Pydantic models) that injects ~20 subsystems into the agentic SSE endpoint /api/generate (main.py:1291). Security is a layered fail-closed spine: gateway.classify (aios/security/gateway.py:283) runs injection→secret→destructive→network→env→shell-escape→scope→caution checks in strict order with RED as the DEFAULT verdict (gateway.py:348), backed by scope_lock canonicalization (scope_lock.py:81), a SHA-256 entropy+regex secret scanner (secret_scanner.py:75), an optional vector injection shield (injection_shield.py:44), a SHA-256 hash-chained audit ledger (audit_logger.py:1-10), and a Docker executor that enforces --network none --read-only --cap-drop ALL --user 65534 --tmpfs noexec plus env-secret stripping (executor.py:197-228, 272-290). Around this core sit demo/evidence HTTP-client drivers (swarm_demo.py, earn_demo.py, curriculum_evidence_driver.py) and a layer of orphaned root prototype scripts (hybrid_search.py, ingest_knowledge.py et al.) that predate aios/memory.
capabilities: Three-zone deterministic security gateway with allowlist-by-default: anything not explicitly GREEN/YELLOW falls through to RED (aios/security/gateway.py:348), and human approval can never authorize a RED action (executor.py:436-443)

Fail-closed scope lock: symlink-resolved real-path canonicalization, shell-operator pre-splitting, Windows drive-aware path detection, and ~/home-reference refusal (aios/security/scope_lock.py:125-177)
SHA-256 hash-chained tamper-evident audit ledger with genesis-from-64-zeros and O(n) verify_chain, isolated from mutable memory (aios/security/audit_logger.py:1-23)

Secret scanner combining named credential regexes (AWS/GitHub/OpenAI/bearer) with a Shannon-entropy (>=4.0 bits/char) second pass, redacting to non-reversible <REDACTED:NAME:hash> tokens (aios/security/secret_scanner.py:28-104)
Docker sandbox executor: ephemeral --rm container, no network, read-only rootfs, all caps dropped, nobody user, pids/memory/cpu limits, noexec tmpfs (aios/core/executor.py:192-237)

Dual-layer prompt-injection defense: deterministic regex plus opt-in cosine-similarity vector blocklist over a curated injection set, fail-safe to regex-only on embedder error (aios/security/injection_shield.py:73-88, gateway.py:306-308)
Per-session RED-action rate limiter that transparently migrates plaintext session ids to SHA-256 keys in its SQLite store (aios/security/gateway.py:178-243)

Guarded T3a self-apply engine that writes the OS's own aios/ source via snapshot→git apply --check→audit→confined apply→integrity check→gated verify→auto-rollback, with no agent-reachable apply tool (aios/core/self_apply.py:1-21)
Sequential role-pass castes (planner→coder→reviewer) with mechanically-enforced per-caste tool subsets (aios/agents/role_pass.py:26-31)
Ephemeral worker swarm: decomposer→per-subtask workers→synthesizer, stigmergic via conversation + sandbox + trail field, no shared mutable state (aios/agents/swarm.py:1-27)
Lazy thread-safe ~90MB MiniLM embedding singleton + persistent FAISS HNSW (inner-product) index with mtime-based reload (aios/memory/embeddings.py:36-105)
UI terminal endpoint that runs commands through the exact same gateway+sandbox+audit path as agent actions, issuing approval tokens for YELLOW (aios/api/main.py:1731-1767)
sophistication: RED-by-default allowlist gateway is genuinely strong: an unknown command is refused, not shelled (gateway.py:348); shell composition metacharacters are rejected even after human approval (gateway.py:119-122, executor.py:297)

The structural no-self-approval guard in self_apply.py is real architecture, not a check: the agent literally has no apply tool (only propose), so applying is reachable ONLY via the human HTTP endpoint; the approved_by!=proposed_by check is an explicit second layer (self_apply.py:13-18)

Executor env sanitization strips any var whose NAME contains KEY/TOKEN/SECRET/PASSWORD/etc plus HOME/USERPROFILE/AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK, then deterministically prepends the project venv to PATH so a bare `python` can't hit a sandbox-planted binary (executor.py:48-51, 272-290, 320-330)
Bounded-output subprocess draining via per-stream threads that read-and-discard past the limit, preventing both unbounded memory and pipe-buffer deadlock (executor.py:86-152)
The vector injection shield is fail-SAFE in the security-correct direction: an embedder failure returns False so the regex layer is never weakened into blocking everything (injection_shield.py:87-88)
Audit-before-write ordering in self_apply: the apply intent is logged to the tamper-evident ledger BEFORE the bytes touch disk, mirroring edit_file/create_file (self_apply.py:6-9)
RateLimiter performs an online schema migration of legacy plaintext session ids into SHA-256 digests on init, merging collisions (gateway.py:185-210)
completeness: High for the live core (~90%+): zero TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX markers exist in live aios/ source — the only matches are the self-analysis detector's own _TODO_MARKER regex (self_analysis_agent.py:66), test fixtures, and the blueprint/assessment reference docs. The security spine, executor, memory, swarm, role-pass, and self-apply engines are all fully implemented, not stubbed. The drag on the number is housekeeping debt, not functional gaps: a dead legacy_node/ tree, a layer of orphaned root prototype scripts, a couple of stray demo/ingest files, untracked sandbox residue, and two large monolith files.
gaps: No documentation of major shipped subsystems: README.md has ZERO mentions of swarm, role-pass/castes, stigmergy, earned-autonomy, trails, or the superbrain frontend (verified via grep — README headers stop at 'Brain Growth Loop' and 'Local Model Gallery'); AGENTS.md has zero mentions of swarm/caste/stigmergy

Host-mode executor explicitly cannot guarantee process-tree containment or network isolation — only the container backend does (executor.py:14-17); the default backend choice determines whether the exfil guardrail is regex-only or true --network none
Two untracked trivial sandbox-residue test files (training_ground/test_auto_grant.py, test_autonomy_live.py — each a 50-byte `assert True` stub) left from live autonomy demos, never cleaned

Stray websocket_security_update.md is a generic Node.js WebSocket-security note irrelevant to this Python/SSE codebase — almost certainly a RAG-ingestion test leftover, documents a transport (WSS/WebSocket) the system does not use
hidden_findings: legacy_node/ (19 files: server.js, securityGateway.js, scopeLock.js, secretScanner.js, auditLogger.js, etc.) is the COMPLETE original Node.js prototype of the entire system — every aios/ security module has a .js ancestor here. It is referenced by NOTHING in aios/tests/frontend (verified) — fully orphaned dead code kept as historical reference (legacy_node/server.js:1-14)

The ~1.3MB frontend bundle is the lazy-loaded Superbrain chunk: frontend/dist/assets/SuperbrainApp-*.js = 1,297,730 bytes — three.js + @react-three/{fiber,drei,postprocessing} + motion all bundled into the 3D scene's code-split chunk. SEPARATELY, the brain 3D assets weigh ~4.5MB of PNG textures (public/textures/brain/normal.png 2.35MB + diffuse.png 1.89MB + specgloss.png 269KB) plus brain.glb 155KB — the real download weight is textures, not JS (frontend/package.json:14-24)

A naming collision: root hybrid_search.py is the DEAD pre-aios prototype (keyed to orchestrator_memory.sqlite / root vector_index.faiss), while aios.api.main ALSO defines a live `hybrid_search` symbol that tests monkeypatch (test_api.py:457). Same name, totally different code — the root one and its siblings (ingest_knowledge.py, ingest_update.py, vector_memory_setup.py, pdf_util.py, extract_text.py) are the orphaned prototype superseded by aios/memory/
chat-ui.html (660 bytes) is a stale hardcoded 'Jarvis' demo HTML with zero connection to the real React frontend — orphaned
success.txt ('OS Online') and creator.txt (0 bytes) are tracked sentinel/marker files with no code references — pure cruft in git

Root SQLite/FAISS artifacts (memory.db 40KB, orchestrator_memory.sqlite 151KB + a once-898KB WAL now checkpointed, vector_index.faiss 65KB, .coverage) sit on disk but are correctly gitignored — they are stale prototype runtime state, NOT tracked
__pycache__ exists in the working tree under every aios/ subpackage and tests/ but is correctly gitignored (verified not tracked) — only a local-disk cleanliness nit
The executor strips AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK BY NAME (executor.py:51) — a Bedrock-specific hardcoding that won't generalize if the cloud provider/credential var changes

Auto-execute GREEN allowlist is extremely narrow — only `echo` and `pwd` match _SAFE_PATTERNS (gateway.py:138-141); literally every other read command (ls, cat, grep) is NOT auto-green and falls to the RED default unless it matches YELLOW — surprisingly strict and easy to misread as permissive
debt: aios/api/main.py is a 1767-line monolith (31 routes, 15 Pydantic models, the entire DI graph, and a ~470-line event_stream generator) — the single largest structural-debt file (aios/api/main.py:1)
aios/agents/tool_agent.py is 1528 lines — the second monolith; the agentic loop, tool dispatch, caste tool-subset enforcement, and grant pre-apply all live in one module (aios/agents/tool_agent.py:1)
frontend/src/App.jsx is 1817 lines and superbrain/superbrain.css is 1825 lines — large single-file UI surfaces that resist incremental review
legacy_node/ (19 files incl. package-lock.json) — dead Node prototype that should be removed or moved out of the tracked tree to stop implying a live JS backend
Tracked cruft to delete: success.txt, creator.txt (0 bytes), chat-ui.html, websocket_security_update.md — none are referenced by any live code
Two untracked 50-byte sandbox-residue test files in training_ground/ (test_auto_grant.py, test_autonomy_live.py) should be cleaned or gitignored — leftover autonomy-demo write-proof

147 broad-except sites in aios/ (non-test), but 65 are explicit `# noqa: BLE001` fail-closed/fail-safe by design (gateway.py:349, scope_lock.py:107, injection_shield.py:87) — deliberate, though the ~80 unannotated ones warrant a sweep to confirm intent

Orphaned root prototype scripts (hybrid_search.py, ingest_knowledge.py, ingest_update.py, vector_memory_setup.py, pdf_util.py, extract_text.py) duplicate functionality now owned by aios/memory and key off the dead orchestrator_memory.sqlite path — confusing to a new reader

Perf hotspot (acceptable but real): first call to any embedding/recall path triggers a ~90MB MiniLM model load + FAISS index read on the request thread via the lazy singleton (aios/memory/embeddings.py:42-56) — cold-start latency on the first /api/generate or memory search

The actual sandbox + exfil guardrail: Docker --network none --read-only --cap-drop ALL container, env-secret stripping, bounded-output draining, structured argv

Documents the security/memory/alignment/curriculum loops but OMITS swarm, role-pass, castes, stigmergy, autonomy, and the superbrain UI — the undocumented-feature gap

purpose: Definitive phase-by-phase status of the shipped code against AI_OS_Blueprint_APlus_v6 (blueprint_text.md) and its companion assessment (aiosv6_assessment_text.md), separating what is genuinely built from genuine gaps (voice, full Neo4j knowledge-graph, Prometheus/Grafana observability) and surplus beyond the blueprint.
architecture: The blueprint defines a 5-phase roadmap (blueprint_text.md:1287-1313) and an 11-stage pipeline (blueprint_text.md:282). The code realizes that pipeline as a deterministic security spine (aios/security/*) wrapping an LLM loop (aios/agents/tool_agent.py), a 4-layer memory (aios/memory/schema.sql) with hybrid retrieval (aios/memory/retrieval.py), and a FastAPI surface (aios/api/main.py) exposing all 8 blueprint REST contracts plus ~24 surplus endpoints. Phases 1-2 are COMPLETE and test-backed; Phase 3 is ~85% (only the knowledge-graph is partial: SQLite triples in facts.py, no Neo4j/traversal); Phase 4 is ~75% (security+rollback+injection-shield done; VOICE entirely absent — zero whisper/piper code in aios/ or requirements.txt); Phase 5 MVP is running (Vite frontend + superbrain), minus the §10 Docker/Prometheus observability stack (only Dockerfile.executor exists). The repo's own §00 audit-reconciliation (blueprint_text.md:154-177) already corrects the blueprint's deliberate understatement, and the shipped reality now far exceeds even that.
capabilities: PHASE 1 (Foundation) COMPLETE: LLM gen, executor, approval flow, file edit all built — aios/core/executor.py, aios/agents/tool_agent.py; blueprint marks these BUILT/P0 (blueprint_text.md:78-92)

PHASE 2 (Memory+Reflection) COMPLETE & test-backed: SQLite L2/L3/L4 (aios/memory/schema.sql:15-112), reflection agent (aios/agents/reflection_agent.py), mistake_pool byte-faithful to blueprint §7.1 (schema.sql:99-112 vs blueprint_text.md:434-449), SHA-256 hash-chain audit (aios/security/audit_logger.py) matching blueprint §7.2 formula (blueprint_text.md:459)

PHASE 2 hybrid retrieval BUILT exactly to blueprint §5.2: aios/memory/retrieval.py:5-10 implements R=alpha*BM25+beta*FAISS+gamma*decay with the exact 0.25/0.45/0.30/0.05 weights via real BM25Okapi (retrieval.py:27,131) + FAISS cosine (retrieval.py:143) + UTC decay

PHASE 3 (Intelligence) ~85%: confidence scoring (aios/core/confidence_filter.py, 0.72 threshold), auto-verify (aios/agents/tool_agent.py _auto_verify), FAISS vector memory (semantic_memory IndexIDMap, schema.sql:83-95) all built

PHASE 3 knowledge-graph PARTIAL (the one genuine Phase-3 gap): aios/memory/facts.py implements Blueprint §5.3 contradiction-aware (subject,predicate,object) triples in flat SQLite (semantic_facts, schema.sql:119-128) — NO graph traversal, NO Neo4j; a 1-hop graph exists only in superseded legacy_node/knowledgeGraph.js, off the product path

PHASE 4 (Security & Voice) ~75%: full gateway+scope_lock+secret_scanner+audit (aios/security/*, 53 tests), rollback engine (aios/agents/rollback_engine.py), prompt-injection shield (aios/security/injection_shield.py) all BUILT — matching blueprint P1/P2

PHASE 4 VOICE = 0% (the largest genuine gap): blueprint §4.2 stage 1 + §12 specify Whisper STT + Piper TTS (blueprint_text.md:287,940-941), but there is NO whisper/piper/pyaudio/transcribe code anywhere in aios/ and NO audio dep in requirements.txt — confirmed by grep; only doc/HUD string mentions exist

PHASE 5 (MVP & Showcase) RUNNING: Vite React frontend (frontend/package.json) + 3D superbrain UI; all 8 blueprint REST endpoints live at exact paths in aios/api/main.py (plan:803, memory/search:508, security/classify:759, approval/req:836, execute:814, reflect:777, audit/verify:770, rollback:876)

SURPLUS beyond blueprint: earned-autonomy evidence->GREEN bridge (aios/core/autonomy.py), self-analysis T0-T4 (aios/agents/self_analysis_agent.py + self_apply.py, designed in assessment §6), stigmergy skill-trails + curriculum + dev-metrics (aios/memory/skills.py,curriculum.py,development.py), worker swarm (aios/agents/swarm.py), Claude+Codex coordination, RAG+CAG, alignment interpreter (aios/core/alignment.py)
sophistication: mistake_pool and the §7.2 hash-chain formula are reproduced byte-for-byte from the blueprint DDL/formula, then hardened beyond it (redact-before-hash, cross-process BEGIN IMMEDIATE) — fidelity plus improvement, not just copying (schema.sql:99-112, aios/security/audit_logger.py)

retrieval.py is a faithful implementation of the blueprint's §5.2 math with EXPLAINABLE per-component sub-scores (faiss/bm25/decay broken out, retrieval.py:143-165) — the blueprint only specified the formula, the code adds transparency

facts.py honors the blueprint §5.3 contradiction-detection ALGORITHM (find conflict on same subject+predicate before commit; surface, never silently accumulate — facts.py:39-106) even though it substitutes SQLite for the planned Neo4j — the intent is met, the backend is downscoped honestly

The shipped system implements the WHOLE assessment §6 self-analysis tier design (T0 index, T1 diagnose, T2 propose-diff, T3 apply, T4 frozen-core-RED) that the blueprint itself never contained — the surplus is itself a faithful build of a separate spec (aiosv6_assessment_text.md:257-411)
completeness: Overall ~80-85% of the blueprint's INTENDED scope, but with large surplus that the blueprint never asked for. Phase 1: 100%. Phase 2: 100% (test-backed; exceeds blueprint §00's pessimistic '~35%'). Phase 3: ~85% (only knowledge-graph partial). Phase 4: ~75% (security 100%, voice 0%). Phase 5: MVP running, observability stack absent. Basis: direct file reads of schema.sql, retrieval.py, facts.py, api/main.py, autonomy.py, Dockerfile.executor; grep confirming voice/Neo4j/Prometheus absence; cross-check against the repo's own AUDIT.md reconciliation and BACKEND_TRUE_PICTURE.md (375 passed/1 skipped baseline). The known starting map (Ph1-2 complete, Ph3 ~85%, Ph4 ~75%, Ph5 MVP) is CONFIRMED.
gaps: VOICE INTERFACE — 0% built. Blueprint §4.2 stage-1 (Whisper+Piper <200ms, blueprint_text.md:287) and §12 toolchain (blueprint_text.md:940-941) specify it; NO STT/TTS/audio code in aios/, NO voice dependency in requirements.txt. This is the single biggest blueprint-vs-reality gap and is correctly deferred (Phase 4, post-internship).

FULL KNOWLEDGE GRAPH (Neo4j + multi-hop traversal) — NOT built. Blueprint §5.1 / §18 Phase-3 (blueprint_text.md:320-322,1300) plan Neo4j entity-relation with multi-hop reasoning. Reality: flat SQLite triple store with contradiction detection only (aios/memory/facts.py, schema.sql:119-128) — no traversal/queries beyond facts_for(subject). The product-path graph is a triple table, not a graph DB.

OBSERVABILITY (Prometheus + Grafana + docker-compose) — NOT built. Blueprint §10 (blueprint_text.md:767-816) specifies an 8-service Docker Compose topology with Prometheus:9090 + Grafana:3001 and 6 key signals. Reality: NO docker-compose.yml, NO prometheus/grafana config anywhere; only Dockerfile.executor (the sandbox image) and an internal JSON endpoint /api/v1/development/metrics:688 — not a metrics-scrape surface.

Diff-preview UI: blueprint §6 promises interactive diff preview at the YELLOW gate; backend approval is capability-based and resumable but the §00 reconciliation (blueprint_text.md:172) flags 'no file-edit diff preview' — verify in frontend; likely still a gap on the UI side.

Sandbox isolation is opt-in not default: blueprint §6.2 implies cgroup-limited sandbox; reality ships a hardened DockerRunner but defaults to host mode (APPROVED_EXECUTION_BACKEND=host) which is explicitly NOT an OS isolation boundary (per executor docstring).
hidden_findings: The blueprint DELIBERATELY UNDERSTATES (blueprint_text.md:149-152 says '~45% implemented, do not claim fully built'), and an inline §00 'Audit Reconciliation' block (blueprint_text.md:154-177) added by Claude Code already flips 6 rows from PARTIAL/DESIGNED to BUILT+tested. So the blueprint is NOT a neutral spec — it's a document that argues against its own pessimism. Reality has moved even further past it.

The companion assessment (aiosv6_assessment_text.md) is not just a review — its §6 (lines 251-411) is the literal DESIGN SOURCE for the self-analysis T0-T4 module that is now fully shipped. Much of the repo's 'surplus' is actually faithful execution of this second spec, including the self_analysis_report table (schema.sql:138-153) modelled on assessment §6.4.

The blueprint specifies 8 REST endpoints (blueprint_text.md:481-521); the running API has ~32 (aios/api/main.py). All 8 originals are present at the exact blueprint paths, so the contract is honored, but the surface is 4x larger — most growth is in /api/v1/development/* (autonomy, skills, trails, curriculum) which the blueprint never contemplated.

A 1-hop knowledge_graph DOES exist (legacy_node/knowledgeGraph.js) — so a reader greping 'knowledge graph' finds a hit and could wrongly conclude the graph is built. It is in the SUPERSEDED Node prototype, a different DB, off the product path. The real product-path 'graph' is facts.py's flat triples.

Earned-autonomy (aios/core/autonomy.py) is the one place the system ATTEMPTS to close the blueprint-never-asked-for 'evidence->autonomy' frontier, but it is OFF by default (AIOS_EARNED_AUTONOMY) and RED is structurally un-earnable — so even the surplus stays inside the blueprint's human-authority invariant (blueprint_text.md:218-221).
debt: The four root RAG scripts and legacy_node/* (incl. knowledgeGraph.js) are SUPERSEDED legacy generation — different DB, naive scoring, off the product path, but a future reader could mistake them for canonical (per BACKEND_TRUE_PICTURE.md:43). The 1-hop knowledge_graph there is the most misleading.

No production document/PDF ingestion pipeline: blueprint memory assumes ingestion, but product knowledge enters only via single-string SemanticMemory.add — the real RAG ingest path is the dead legacy scripts (BACKEND_TRUE_PICTURE.md:43).

Audit chain has no external anchoring (blueprint §7.2 hash-chain is local-only): an attacker with full write access can recompute a self-consistent forged chain — inherent to a single-laptop hash chain, flagged in §00 reconciliation context and BACKEND_TRUE_PICTURE.md:43,58.

Observability claimed in blueprint §10 with concrete alert rules and SLOs (blueprint_text.md:799-823) but zero implementing artifacts — the alert-rule tables are documentation-only and could read as 'built' to a skimmer.

Growth stores have no forgetting/compaction (episodic append-only, semantic only superseded) — not a blueprint requirement but a real long-run debt that the blueprint's TTL columns (§5.1) imply and the code does not enforce.
